known_gaps: External guideline artifacts are not cached or license-verified.
Clinical and safety review is incomplete.
Simulation set is below release coverage requirements.

event.syncope.during_exertion.wording: 운동이나 격한 활동을 하는 도중에 의식을 잃었나요?

event.syncope.lateral_tongue_bite.wording: 의식을 잃은 뒤 혀 옆부분을 깨문 상처가 있었나요?

event.syncope.limb_jerking.wording: 의식을 잃은 동안 팔다리가 반복적으로 떨리거나 경직되었다는 말을 들었나요?

event.syncope.no_prodrome.wording: 식은땀, 메스꺼움, 더운 느낌, 눈앞이 캄캄함 같은 전조 없이 갑자기 의식을 잃었나요?

event.syncope.post_event_confusion.wording: 깨어난 뒤 한동안 혼란스럽거나 평소 상태로 돌아오는 데 오래 걸렸나요?

event.syncope.reflex_prodrome.wording: 쓰러지기 전에 식은땀, 더운 느낌, 메스꺼움 또는 눈앞이 캄캄한 증상이 있었나요?

event.syncope.reflex_trigger.wording: 오래 서 있기, 통증, 공포, 채혈이나 의료 시술 직후에 증상이 생겼나요?

family_history.sudden_cardiac_death_unde.wording: 가까운 가족이 40세 이전에 갑자기 심장 문제로 사망했거나 유전성 심장질환이 있나요?

history.cardiac_disease.wording: 부정맥, 심장판막질환, 심근병증, 심근경색 또는 심장 시술 병력이 있나요?

history.diabetes.wording: 당뇨병이 있거나 혈당을 낮추는 약을 사용하나요?

medication.current.wording: 현재 복용 중인 약이 있나요? 특히 혈압약, 이뇨제, 심장약을 포함해 알려주세요.

symptom.chest_pain.wording: 쓰러지거나 어지러운 전후에 가슴 통증이나 압박감이 있었나요?

symptom.dizziness.head_movement_trigger.wording: 고개를 돌리거나 눕고 일어날 때 짧게 빙빙 도는 증상이 생기나요?

symptom.dizziness.postural_trigger.wording: 누워 있거나 앉아 있다가 일어설 때 증상이 생기거나 심해지나요?

symptom.dizziness.presentation.wording: 어지럼은 빙빙 도는 느낌, 쓰러질 듯한 느낌, 중심을 못 잡는 느낌, 실제 의식소실 중 무엇에 가깝나요?

symptom.dizziness.severity.wording: 현재 증상은 가벼움, 중간, 서거나 걷기 어려울 정도 중 어디에 가깝나요?

symptom.dyspnea.wording: 새로 숨이 차거나 숨쉬기 어렵나요? 가벼움, 중간, 심함으로 답해도 됩니다.

symptom.hearing_change_or_tinnitus.wording: 청력이 달라지거나 귀가 먹먹하거나 이명이 함께 있나요?

symptom.neurological_deficit.wording: 갑자기 한쪽 힘이 빠짐, 얼굴 비대칭, 말하기 어려움, 겹쳐 보임 또는 새 감각 이상이 있나요?

symptom.new_gait_unsteadiness.wording: 갑자기 새로 똑바로 서거나 걷기 어려워졌나요?

symptom.palpitations.wording: 증상 전후에 심장이 매우 빠르거나 불규칙하게 뛰는 느낌이 있었나요?

symptom.syncope.full_recovery.wording: 의식을 잃었다면 지금은 평소처럼 완전히 회복되었나요?

elements.limitation: Clinical sex used for interview routing is not automatically equivalent to every USCDI sex-related element.

elements.data_element: Medication, Dose, Dose Unit, Indication and Dispense Status
Blood Pressure, Heart Rate, Respiratory Rate, Temperature and Pulse Oximetry
Laboratory Test, Result, Result Status and Specimen
Clinical Test and Result or Report
Patient Goals and Care Experience Preference
Encounter Type, Time, Location and Disposition
Care Team Member Identity, Role and Contact
Medication, Laboratory, Imaging and Clinical Test Orders
History and Physical or Progress Note

limitations: Coverage indicates candidate data-population capability, not US certification or conformance.
A mapped Fact does not make the data element clinically required for this encounter.
US-specific vocabularies and regulatory semantics do not replace Korean bindings or policy.

elements.display: Patient concern, goal and expected outcome
Pain presence and NRS score when pain applies
Patient, proxy or record information source

nodes.display: Full Recovery after Loss of Consciousness

guideline_behavior.rationale: Interview behavior derived from guidelines may change with recommendations.

local_jurisdiction.rationale: Local pathways and administrative rules may change quickly.

safety_critical.rationale: Safety behavior and escalation knowledge require frequent monitoring.

stable_semantic.rationale: Atomic patient-reportable concepts are relatively stable but still require periodic review.

terminology_mapping.rationale: Terminology and interoperability baselines have independent releases.

clinical_guideline.rationale: Daily automated monitoring provides early detection for high-impact guideline changes; full review occurs only when change is detected or the review date is reached.

interoperability_standard.rationale: Versioned interoperability standards and domain datasets are monitored weekly; detected changes trigger mapping and Coverage review without changing clinical rules.

nice_guidance.rationale: NICE guidance is versioned and may be amended; weekly monitoring detects changes without implying weekly clinical re-review.

public_health_guidance.rationale: Public-health pages can change between formal releases; weekly monitoring is appropriate for the research package.

terminology_server.rationale: Terminology servers publish versioned content; monthly baseline monitoring balances stability and acquisition cost.

assertions: Sudden-onset dizziness with focal neurological deficit, new unsteadiness or new deafness requires immediate stroke-pathway assessment unless an explained benign presentation or corrected hypoglycaemia accounts for it.
Sudden acute vestibular syndrome includes vertigo, nausea or vomiting and gait unsteadiness and requires trained bedside assessment or immediate referral when a benign cause is not established.
Dizziness without imbalance or focal neurological deficit is less likely to represent a serious neurological condition.
Head-movement triggered transient rotational vertigo, postural symptoms and recurrent headache association modify subsequent assessment without establishing a diagnosis.
artifacts.title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral — dizziness and vertigo in adults

assertions: Incomplete recovery or injury after transient loss of consciousness requires urgency assessment.

Urgent cardiovascular assessment is indicated with ECG abnormality, heart failure, TLoC during exertion, family history of sudden cardiac death under 40 or inherited cardiac disease, unexplained breathlessness or heart murmur.
Event history records posture, provoking factors, prodrome, appearance, movements, tongue biting, injury, duration and recovery confusion.

Strong seizure-associated features warrant urgent neurological assessment, while prolonged standing, pain or procedure triggers and warmth or sweating prodrome can support a simple-faint pattern only after alternative warning features are excluded.
artifacts.title: Transient loss of consciousness (blackouts) in over 16s

assertions: STOM confirmed active 404640003 Dizziness (finding) and 271594007 Syncope (finding).
Finding site and Severity were allowed for Dizziness; the Syncope allowed-attribute query returned an empty collection, so no post-coordinated Syncope attribute is asserted.
MRCM validates terminology modeling only and does not determine clinical questions, priority, diagnosis or safety.
artifacts.title: STOM SNOMED CT lookup and MRCM allowed attributes for dizziness and syncope
